instruction: Implement missing CRUD API endpoints for the RulesEngineEditor backend to support the frontend's workflow and scenario management features.

context.database: SQL Server with EF Core (code-first, auto-generated entities)
context.auth: JWT Bearer + ASP.NET Core Identity (currently [AllowAnonymous] on RulesController)
context.frontend_contract: The React frontend calls these endpoints via axios with relative paths. Vite proxy rewrites /rules to /api/Rules and /scenarios to /api/Rules/scenarios.

existing_endpoints: POST /api/Rules/scenarios - Accepts SaveScenarioRequest, returns WorkflowTestScenarios (201 Created)

workflow_crud.description: Get a single workflow definition by ID, including full JsonContent

workflow_crud.frontend_usage: apiClient.saveWorkflow(workflowDef) - saves new workflow from editor

workflow_crud.description: Delete a workflow definition by ID

scenario_crud.description: List all scenarios, optionally filtered by workflowDefinitionId query parameter
scenario_crud.frontend_usage: apiClient.getScenarios(workflowId) - loads scenarios for selected workflow

scenario_crud.description: Get a single scenario by ID

WorkflowDefinitionResponse.notes: Full workflow response including JsonContent (the raw rules JSON string)

CreateWorkflowRequest.validation: JsonContent must be valid JSON. WorkflowName + Version must be unique (UQ_Workflow_Version).

UpdateWorkflowRequest.validation: If JsonContent provided, must be valid JSON. If WorkflowName/Version changed, must not violate UQ_Workflow_Version.

properties: string? MockInputJson (must be valid JSON if provided)
string? ExpectedOutputJson (must be valid JSON if provided)
UpdateScenarioRequest.notes: WorkflowDefinitionId should NOT be updatable (scenarios belong to one workflow)

methods: Task<WorkflowDefinitions> CreateWorkflowAsync(WorkflowDefinitions workflow, CancellationToken ct = default)
Task<WorkflowDefinitions?> UpdateWorkflowAsync(int id, WorkflowDefinitions workflow, CancellationToken ct = default)
Task<bool> DeleteWorkflowAsync(int id, CancellationToken ct = default)
Task<List<WorkflowTestScenarios>> GetScenariosAsync(int? workflowId = null, CancellationToken ct = default)
Task<WorkflowTestScenarios?> GetScenarioByIdAsync(int id, CancellationToken ct = default)
Task<WorkflowTestScenarios?> UpdateScenarioAsync(int id, WorkflowTestScenarios scenario, CancellationToken ct = default)
Task<bool> DeleteScenarioAsync(int id, CancellationToken ct = default)

notes: Use EF Core DbSet operations (Add, Update, Remove, SaveChangesAsync)
For GetScenariosAsync: filter by workflowId if provided, order by ScenarioName
For Update methods: load existing entity, apply changes, save. Return null if not found.
For Delete methods: return true if deleted, false if not found.

actions: GET api/Rules/{id} - call repository.GetWorkflowByIdAsync, map to WorkflowDefinitionResponse
POST api/Rules - validate request, create entity, save, return 201 with WorkflowDefinitionResponse
PUT api/Rules/{id} - load existing, apply changes, save, return 200 with WorkflowDefinitionResponse
GET api/Rules/scenarios - accept workflowId query param, return List<ScenarioResponse>
PUT api/Rules/scenarios/{id} - validate JSON fields, update, return ScenarioResponse
validation_rules: JsonContent must be valid JSON for Create and Update workflow
MockInputJson and ExpectedOutputJson must be valid JSON for Create and Update scenario

implementation_tasks.notes: Add all new endpoints and schemas to both YAML and JSON OpenAPI specs. The frontend uses these for reference.

frontend_api_contract.notes: The frontend apiClient.js expects these exact response shapes:
frontend_api_contract.getWorkflows: Returns { isMock: false, data: [...] } where data is array of workflow objects
frontend_api_contract.getWorkflow: Returns { isMock: false, data: workflowObject } or { isMock: false, data: null } for 404
frontend_api_contract.saveWorkflow: Returns { isMock: false, data: createdWorkflow } with WorkflowDefinitionId assigned
frontend_api_contract.deleteWorkflow: Returns { isMock: false, data: { success: true } }
frontend_api_contract.getScenarios: Returns { isMock: false, data: [...] } filtered by workflowId if provided
frontend_api_contract.getScenario: Returns { isMock: false, data: scenarioObject } or { isMock: false, data: null }
frontend_api_contract.saveScenario: Returns { isMock: false, data: createdScenario } with ScenarioId assigned (201)
frontend_api_contract.deleteScenario: Returns { isMock: false, data: { success: true } }

critical_constraints: The database uses EF Core with auto-generated entities. DO NOT modify entity classes manually - they are generated by EF Core Power Tools.
The existing unique constraint UQ_Workflow_Version on (WorkflowName, Version) must be respected.
JsonContent column is stored as JSON string in SQL Server (column type: json).
All datetime values should be UTC.
The frontend falls back to localStorage mock data when backend returns 404/500. Ensure endpoints return proper HTTP status codes.
Maintain [AllowAnonymous] on RulesController for now - auth will be added in a future phase.
Use CancellationToken parameters on all async methods (existing pattern).
Follow existing code style: sealed classes, primary constructors, record DTOs.
testing_requirements: Add unit tests for new repository methods in RulesEngineEditor.Server.UnitTests
Add integration tests for new controller endpoints
Test the unique constraint violation scenario for workflows
Test JSON validation for JsonContent, MockInputJson, ExpectedOutputJson
files_to_create: New DTO records in RulesController.cs or separate DTO files
Unit tests for new repository methods
Integration tests for new controller endpoints
